title: Tariffs: EU agrees to implement US trade pact
subhead: EU lawmakers and member states reached a deal in the early hours of Wednesday to implement the bloc's nearly year-old trade pact with the United States. President Donald Trump had threatened to impose new tariffs unless it was completed by July 4.

title: Cuba's crackdown on dissidents continues, despite US pressure
French Ministry of Culture taps Studios Architecture Paris and Selldorf Architects to lead Louvre transformation
'Heat strokes are part of the job': In Delhi, construction workers endure 43°C heat
subhead: India's capital has faced scorching days since April. On construction sites, workers have tried to adapt but many have fallen victim to heatstroke.

title: Arsenal wins Premier League, ending 22-year wait
subhead: The day after their victory over Burnley, the Gunners clinched their 14th league title as Manchester City failed to win at Bournemouth. The triumph delivers long-awaited relief for the North London club's supporters.

title: Cannes 2026: 'Moulin' brings the ordeal of a French Resistance hero harrowingly to life
subhead: Laszlo Nemes chose to focus his film on the confrontation between French resistance hero Jean Moulin and Klaus Barbie, the head of the Lyon Gestapo, using an immersive approach that raises questions.

title: The EU's unflinching collaboration with Libya and Tunisia to contain the flow of migrants
subhead: 'Migrants in the Mediterranean: The mechanics of silence' (2/4). Giorgia Meloni's government, backed by the European Union, is funding, equipping and training both the Libyan and Tunisian coast guards – even though these forces have committed rights abuses – to intercept people in exile on their way to Europe.

Greenland remains under US pressure
subhead: The American president's envoy, Louisiana Governor Jeff Landry, has embarked on a three-day visit to Nuuk, as the United States seeks to expand its presence on the island.

title: Gaza's vanished past lives on through photographs accidentally rediscovered in Marseille
subhead: Kegham Djeghalian, a photographer and Armenian genocide survivor, settled in Gaza in 1944. He documented the Palestinian territory through portraits and reports until he died in 1981. His grandson has chosen to exhibit his images of ordinary and joyful moments in Gaza, now devastated by two years of war.

title: Captured, imprisoned and tortured: Russia 'ignores all laws and does whatever it wants' to Ukrainian civilians
subhead: Since the 2022 invasion, Moscow has been arresting citizens from the occupied territories en masse in an effort to crush all resistance. More than 16,000 people are currently imprisoned, according to the Ukrainian Parliament Commissioner for Human Rights.
